title: rosa canina subsp. collina
intro: Rosa canina subsp. collina, also known as the dwarf dog rose or alpine dog rose, is a subspecies of the dog rose plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This shrub has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich source of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, particularly in its hips, which contain high levels of vitamin C and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subsp. collina include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina subsp. collina, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide a natural remedy for various health issues.
